How it works Features Security Live mesh Builders About Get One Link

we are one

Send anything. To anyone. Only you can read it.

A free, private network for your messages, files, and devices. No accounts. No servers in the middle. No limits. It just works.

Get One Link → See how it works
1,247 nodes online site verified checking pq session deriving
click anywhere to send a pulse
Three promises

The things every other app forgot.

Built so you never have to read a privacy policy. The architecture is the promise.

No accounts. Ever.

No email. No phone number. No signup. Open the app and it works. Your device is your identity. Nothing about you leaves your hands.

No servers in the middle.

Messages and files go straight from one device to the other. Nothing stored anywhere. If we vanished tomorrow, your One Link still works.

Free. Forever.

No tier. No subscription. No enterprise plan. No premium. The source is open. You can read every line. You can run your own.

Try it right now

Pair your phone in five seconds.

Scan this code with One Link on your phone. Read the five words back. You are paired. No download yet, no signup, no permissions. Just a real handshake, right here.

Verify these five words match what your phone shows.

orbit amber river candle harbor

That is the entire pairing flow. No password. No backup phrase. The five words mean the math agreed on both ends. If anyone tried to sit in the middle, the words would not match.

or pair with another browser tab right now →

What just happened, under the hood
handshake
X25519 + ML-KEM-768 hybrid
verification
5-word SAS derived from the shared secret
identity
Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 hybrid signature
field witness
environment witness mixed in to bind to physical context
crate
ol_pair_qr (compiled to WASM for in-browser handshake)
Why this exists

The internet was supposed to be ours.

Somewhere along the way it became a place where five companies hold every message, every photo, every file you ever made. One Link is the quiet way back.

Join the network → Read the covenant
One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
